narrative: OMX30 opens Monday in a structural bull market—up 8.7% from its 200-day MA with robust ADX 44.7 signaling strong directional momentum. Overnight, US 10Y yields rose 51bp to 4.33%, lifting bank NIM expectations but capping tech/growth multiples. Commodity divergence: Brent crude crashed 2.98% (easing industrials margin pressure), while copper firmed 0.58%, directly benefiting Boliden and mining. Currency headwinds are modest: USD/SEK weakened 52bp, slightly unfavorable for Volvo/Atlas Copco exporters. Technicals remain bullish—MACD above signal, ROC-90 at +9.6%—but RSI 64.26 and proximity to 52-week highs (83% of range, -4.4% from peak) warrant caution on mean reversion. Key risk: escalating China-US tensions and geopolitical noise could trigger quick VIX spike; watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for macro confirmation.
morningBrief: OMX30 set for modest +0.3–0.5% open on resilient technical setup: bull market structure intact, MACD positive, ADX strong at 44.7. Overnight yields +51bp favor banks (SEB, Handelsbanken wider NIMs) but weigh on tech/small-caps. Copper +0.58% is a small positive for Boliden; oil's -2.98% crash eases Volvo/Atlas input-cost fears. USD/SEK down 52bp is a minor headwind for exporters—monitor if it weakens further. Sector calls: **Banks OVERWEIGHT** (rates up), **Industrials NEUTRAL-POSITIVE** (oil down offsets export headwind), **Tech UNDERWEIGHT** (growth multiple compression), **Mining WATCH** (copper constructive but modest). Support: 3085 (lower Bollinger); resistance 3180 (50-MA). Watch US Jobless Claims 14:30 CET—any surprise weakness could rattle sentiment. China-Meta/geopolitical news flow remains a tail risk for volatility spikes.

USD/SEK weakening (-0.52%) slightly headwind for exporters; EUR/USD strength (+0.44%) mixed signal for eurozone-exposed names
US yields rising (+0.51%) supports banks but pressures growth/tech; VIX modest at 18.77 suggests no panic
Geopolitical/trade headlines (China-Meta, UAE-Pakistan) create uncertainty, but OMX30 technicals remain constructive
